verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase that is N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, targeting it to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane — its canonical location is inner-leaflet-anchored. However, multiple studies document that LYN and other Src-family kinases are detectable on the extracellular face of tumor cells (cancer ecto-kinase phenomenon). This oncogenic-state ecto-display qualifies as cell_state_induced surface accessibility. The baseline biology is inner-leaflet anchored, but the cancer-cell ecto-pool is a documented non-baseline surface story.
